title: Malty Korean Onion Bao
description: Soft, malty, and savory, these Korean baos are a perfect post-workout treat. Made with double-rise bread and featuring a filling of caramelized yellow onion and spices.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the gluten-free flour, sugar, and salt.
Add the dry ingredients to the yeast mixture and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Form the dough into a ball and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Use a rolling pin to flatten each ball of dough into a disk, about 1/4 inch thick.
In a separate pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chopped green onion and cook for another minute until softened.
Add the caramelized yellow onion and gochujang. Cook for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Brush the edges of the dough disks with a little water, then place a spoonful of the onion mixture in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Cook the baos in a non-stick skillet or steamer for 5-7 minutes until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Store any leftover baos in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Reheat frozen baos in the microwave or oven before serving.
